signals.reasoning: BTC still has a broadly constructive structure, but veto is active and the move is already well off the early trigger point. That keeps it in watch-only mode rather than a fresh long.

ETH is the strongest structure on the board, with trend-supported price and open-interest expansion plus still-negative funding, but the active veto headline is enough to keep even the best setup from becoming an actionable long.
SOL still looks more like a short-covering rally than a clean bearish continuation, but the board is vetoed and there is no fresh edge to force here either. Watch only.
GRIFFAIN reads like a completed long-liquidation flush, not a fresh short with easy follow-through. Under veto that means pass rather than chase.
TST remains in an active squeeze with very large price and open-interest expansion, but it is already too crowded and too extended to chase cleanly while the event brake is active.

CC has real squeeze-long characteristics from negative funding and continued upside, but the move is already underway and open-interest history is too incomplete to trust it as a fresh entry under veto.
MERL still looks constructive with price and open interest both up, but it is not a special enough setup to override the active event brake.
FARTCOIN is another active continuation name, but the move is already crowded and extended. Under veto this is watch-only, not a fresh long.
RESOLV has a mixed setup: mild trend support, but also exchange-notice overhang from the Binance monitoring-tag story. That is not clean enough to justify action during veto.
LDO is one of the cleaner continuation longs on raw structure, but it is already stretched after the rebound and still does not clear the veto bar for a fresh trade.
KNEIRO has upside momentum, but the chart is extended and the supporting open-interest history is incomplete. It stays on the sidelines.
